title: Unable to pull Docker registry image on Fedora Rawhide (Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not supported)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

While trying to pull an image through `podman run` the command fails with:
```
Error: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:6eda6749503f60eb69077e148cba1015ec5808b0c1c2e6f027f7d1b960f6a3a9": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not supported
```

The affected command is:
```
# Create a Docker registry without authentication
run $PODMAN --root "$BATS_RUN_TMPDIR"/reg run -d \
    --rm \
    --name docker-registry-noauth \
    --privileged \
    -v "$BATS_RUN_TMPDIR"/certs:/certs \
    -e REGISTRY_HTTP_ADDR=0.0.0.0:443 \
    -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_CERTIFICATE=/certs/domain.crt \
    -e REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_KEY=/certs/domain.key \
    -p 50000:443 \
    docker.io/library/registry:2
```

The command is ran as part of system tests ran using [bats-core](https://github.com/bats-core/bats-core). In the `certs` dir are certificates needed for HTTPS. That part can be left out.

The tests are executed on hosts with Fedora 33, Fedora 34 and Fedora Rawhide. I encountered https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10038 (there are more reports for the same issue) along the way.

I could not reproduce the issue locally (Fedora Silverblue Rawhide with the same version of Podman).

Whole output of the affected command:
```
Trying to pull docker.io/library/registry:2...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:ddad3d7c1e96adf9153f8921a7c9790f880a390163df453be1566e9ef0d546e0
Copying blob sha256:5b94580856e6fd27a74d55ab33fc3b53fd6f599f7147f38845a2482cc003cc19
Copying blob sha256:12008541203a024dab3a30542c87421e5a27dbb601b9c6f029b3827af138ccb3
Copying blob sha256:363ab70c2143bc121f037ba432cede225b7053200abba2a7a4ca24c2915a3998
Copying blob sha256:6eda6749503f60eb69077e148cba1015ec5808b0c1c2e6f027f7d1b960f6a3a9
Copying config sha256:1fd8e1b0bb7ef6cc63e0094e2fb5a35259bdce7041ef5e7e4c99694ded1041d7
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:6eda6749503f60eb69077e148cba1015ec5808b0c1c2e6f027f7d1b960f6a3a9": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not supported
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run --rm --privileged docker.io/library/registry:2`

**Describe the results you received:**

Error when trying to run Docker registry in a container. The root directory with overlays and such cannot be removed by user.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Successfully launched Docker registry in a container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I applied the following [fix](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10038#issuecomment-820185033) that has been used successfully. It helped with jobs running on Fedora 33 and Fedora 34 hosts but not here. The two environments have both older versions of Podman and fuse-overlayfs.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.0-dev
API Version:  3.3.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.16.4
Built:        Fri Jun 11 04:17:43 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Link to affected CI job: https://softwarefactory-project.io/zuul/t/local/build/e0f0e7624d1943659f42232de67f40d0 (switch to tab `Console` for more practical and informal view).

comments.body: Do we have any more details on the CI environment - is Podman running in a VM or a container? Are there any security constraints on the CI environment, e.g. a seccomp profile that could block a syscall we want to use?

Podman runs in a VM. The CI is used for testing Toolbox and that can not be done inside of a container.

I'm not aware of any abnormalities in the setup but my knowledge of the CI environment is very limited.
@TristanCacqueray might know a bit more about some possible limitations in the CI environment.

> This is rootless Podman correct?

Indeed.

This smells like the linger issues that people often have.   But the Operation not supported, does not match the usually issue.

@giuseppe Any thoughts?  One difference in Rawhide, is that it uses native overlayfs rather then fuse-overlayfs.
This is happening with an OpenStack instance started with the fedora cloud image. I don't think there is any special security constraints.

> One difference in Rawhide, is that it uses native overlayfs rather then fuse-overlayfs.

I believe I forced the use of `fuse-overlayfs` by applying https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10038#issuecomment-820185033.

Without applying the workaround the job still [fails](https://softwarefactory-project.io/zuul/t/local/build/f813e7bb53204b499208074007a72159) with the same error and now even the jobs running on Fedora 33 and 34 fail with the same error as above. Output of `podman info --debug`:


So we fail both with, and without, mount program set, with the same error?

I guess "$BATS_RUN_TMPDIR" is pointing to `/tmp` or another tmpfs that doesn't support extended attributes.

The error above happens when setting an extended attribute, to reproduce it:

```
$ touch /tmp/foo
$ setfattr -n user.foo -v bar /tmp/foo
setfattr: /tmp/foo: Operation not supported
```

Please use another file system for the storage root.

I am closing the issue but please keep commenting and re-open it if I am wrong and that is not the reason.

That sounds like the cause of the problem. Thanks for the insight, @giuseppe!

I wonder though why this issue happens only on Rawhide and not the stable versions of Fedora.

Anyway, I'll try to use a different location for the root.

> I wonder though why this issue happens only on Rawhide and not the stable versions of Fedora.
> 
> Anyway, I'll try to use a different location for the root.

that happens because Fedora Rawhide has support for native overlay also for rootless.  In this case whiteout files are created using extended attributes, while on the older Fedora version we were using a different format for whiteout files (`.wh.FILE`) that is used by fuse-overlayfs
